title: Profiling, Allocator Performance, and Hardening · Changed-Lifetime Lab
summary: An independent maintainer ports the measured allocator optimization report to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of measured allocator optimization report with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry.
Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.
Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Allocation Telemetry
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 2665. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Allocation Telemetry.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure allocation rate, size distribution, lifetime, peak live bytes, failures, and call sites with bounded telemetry. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Allocation Telemetry, keep Allocation Telemetry connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Optimization Preserves Contract symptom in measured allocator optimization report during this lab case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: mimalloc Documentation — Allocator design, statistics, secure modes, performance claims, and workload measurement.

hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Optimization Preserves Contract, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Optimization Preserves Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Optimization Preserves Contract, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A lab native-systems team handles memory case 9630. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Optimization Preserves Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A faster benchmark permits weaker cleanup or failure behavior.

stimulus.title: Optimization Preserves Contract failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A faster benchmark permits weaker cleanup or failure behavior.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Optimization Preserves Contract case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe. This does not satisfy Optimization Preserves Contract for measured allocator optimization report.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Optimization Preserves Contract condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Optimization Preserves Contract output and hide the violated measured allocator optimization report assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Optimization Preserves Contract.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Optimization Preserves Contract
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Optimization Preserves Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 2249. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Optimization Preserves Contract.
steps.why: Retrieving Optimization Preserves Contract through the concrete measured allocator optimization report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Optimization Preserves Contract, keep Optimization Preserves Contract connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require functional, sanitizer, resource, load, and changed-workload evidence before accepting a memory optimization.
